reasoning: The load-bearing divergence holds: the ROM colours each drawn TIE piece from its remaining life-timer (VWTIN, WSXPLD.MAC:760-772 — LDB XP$TMR(X); LSLB; LDD B(U) into TVWCLE), so the piece walks a colour/intensity ramp as it counts down; our render draws all three fragments in one static TIE_GLOW with no time variation (render.ts:394-396, verified). Citations are accurate: :764 CMPB #1F, :766 LDD #VJFLS ';THEN REALLY FLASH', :768 LDU #TVWCLE. TVWCLE (:787-790) is a hand-authored bright->dim table, so the 'cooling as it dies' read is right. HOWEVER the title/claim's 'flash white THEN colour-cycle' is FALSE for TIE pieces and must be struck (see corrections).

corrections.flash_branch_never_taken_for_TIE_pieces: VWTIN takes the VJFLS 'REALLY FLASH' branch only when XP$TMR > #1F (hex 1F = 31 decimal; CMPB #1F / IFHI at :764-766). But every TIE explosion piece is born with a timer BELOW that threshold: both wings load LDA #18 = 0x18 = 24 (BGAXP, WSXPLD.MAC:165 and :196) and the centre globe loads LDA #10 = 0x10 = 16 (:224). 24 and 16 are both <= 31 and the timer only ever DEC-rements (DOXPLD), so it never rises above 0x1F. Therefore TIE pieces NEVER flash — they index TVWCLE from birth and only colour-cycle down. The 'flash white then color-cycle' framing is wrong for the TIE case this finding is scoped to. The VJFLS flash path is reachable only by ground-object (tower/bunker) explosions, whose timer is #20 = 0x20 = 32 > 31 (per X-005) — a different, out-of-scope explosion type. Net: the divergence is purely the MISSING age-keyed colour/intensity ramp (real, holds); the claimed initial white flash is not part of TIE-piece behaviour. class/recommendation (DIVERGENCE / fix size m) stand once the flash clause is removed.

reasoning: Fully verified against WSXPLD.MAC:805-976. VWXPLN dispatches XP.PHS through TXPPHS (:811 .WORD XP.NUL,XP.PH0,XP.PH1,XP.PH2,XP.PH3) and the four handlers cycle colour exactly as claimed: PH0 (:816) 'FROM DIM TO BRIGHT' RED circles — LDD #VGCOLR+<VGCRED*100>, cited line :817 verbatim-correct; PH1 (:837) 'BRIGHT BLUE FROM CENTER, RED EXPANSION' — VGCBLU circles (:839) + VGCRED DRING (:879-880); PH2 (:896) 'BRIGHT WHITE FROM CENTER, BLUE EXPANSION' — VGCWHT circles (:898) + VGCBLU DRING (:939-940); PH3 (:955) 'CONTINUALLY EXPANDING WHITE RINGS' — VGCWHT DRING (:970-971). Colour equates confirmed in WSGLOB.MAC: VGCBLU==1 (:56), VGCRED==4 (:59), VGCWHT==7 (:62). Geometry is ONLY DCIRCL (concentric circles) + DRING (scaling rings) — no radial rays anywhere. Ours (drawDeathStarBoom) draws a single amber '#ffdd66' (render.ts:566, verified), BOOM_RINGS=3 concentric rings (:559) PLUS a BOOM_RAYS=16 ray starburst (:560) that has no ROM counterpart. Red->blue->white multi-phase arc and pure-ring geometry both genuinely diverge from our single-amber ring+ray stand-in. No corrections.

The core claim holds and is now pinnable. Our comment (render.ts:843-846, verified) states 'no ROM timing exists' and pins DEATH_STAR_BOOM_SECONDS = 2.5, which also drives the drawDeathStarBoom animation length — but the ROM finale duration IS deterministically defined by XP.CNT counter arithmetic per game frame (WSXPLD.MAC): PH1 XP.CNT+=2 to >=0x3F, PH2 +=3 to >=0x50 then reload 0x80, PH3 -=4 down to <0x08. Decoded frame counts (one VWXPLN dispatch per 20.508 Hz game frame): PH1 = 31 frames, PH2 = 27 frames, PH3 = 31 frames = 89 frames for the ring sequence alone = 89/20.508 = 4.34 s, on top of the DX1 enlarge prelim (X-007) — materially longer than a single 2.5 s ramp. Claim (ROM defines the duration via counters; it is clearly > 2.5 s) survives. recommendation accept stands.

corrections.exact_frame_count_now_decoded: The finding declined an exact figure, citing the PH0 IFLO/IFHS anomaly. That anomaly is real but immaterial: PH0's IFLO block (WSXPLD.MAC:824-828) ADVANCES the phase (resets XP.CNT=1, INC XP.PHS) whenever XP.CNT<0x3F, so with XP.CNT seeded below 0x3F it advances after ~1 frame — PH0 contributes only ~1 frame, not a long red-circle phase. PH1/PH2/PH3 use the clean IFLO-draw / IFHS-advance pattern and give 31/27/31 = 89 frames. So the ring animation is ~89-90 frames ~= 4.3 s at 20.508 Hz (assuming one dispatch per game frame), refining the finding's 'order of several seconds' to a concrete value and reinforcing '> 2.5 s'.
corrections.comment_is_overbroad_not_simply_absent: 'no ROM timing exists' is defensible for a text 'DESTROYED' banner dwell (the ROM has no such banner), but DEATH_STAR_BOOM_SECONDS also governs the explosion ANIMATION, which DOES have a ROM counterpart (the 4-phase ring sequence). The comment is overbroad rather than merely a UX guess.

reasoning: The 7/8 per-application decay is genuinely faithful and correctly decoded. MOVAM (WSGUNS.MAC:484-488) computes, per axis, CLRD / SUBD 0(U) / JSR ASRD3 / ADDD 0(U) / STD 0(U) = pos - pos/8 = pos*(7/8). ASRD3 = ARITHMETIC-SHIFT-RIGHT-D by 3 = /8, verified from the .IRP unroll in WSMATH.MAC:62-67 (labels ASRD8..ASRD0 fall through; entering at ASRD3 executes ASRA/RORB three times then ASRD0 RTS). The stale comment 'POS-(POS/4)' (:485) is correctly flagged: the code calls ASRD3 (/8), not ASRD2 (/4), so the executed factor is 7/8 and our sim ports the executed factor. Shot stored relative to the player (:172 'POSITION SHOT RELATIVE TO PLAYER'), so decay toward 0 homes on the cockpit at origin — matches our sim (fireball vel [0,0,0], position scaled by the 7/8 decay each tick). Our ratio (sim.ts:1081 Math.pow(7/8, dt*TICK_HZ)) is correct per application. But per the coverage-review charge, the 'per game frame' MATCH holds only per-tick, and the tick is wrong — see corrections.

corrections.match_holds_per_tick_not_at_the_governin: MOVAM runs once per 20.508 Hz game frame, so the ROM applies 7/8 exactly 20.508 times/second. Our code applies it dt*TICK_HZ times/second with TICK_HZ=30 (state.ts:220) = 30 times/second. 30/20.508 = 1.463x, so the effective homing decay is ~1.46x too aggressive: our fireball converges onto the cockpit measurably faster than the cabinet's. The finding's title 'decays x7/8 per game frame' is only faithful if TICK_HZ = 20.508; as shipped our 'frame' is 1/30 s, not the ROM's 1/20.508 s game frame. The finding honestly defers the cadence to G-003, so this remains CONFIRMED (ratio real, correctly ported) rather than a kill — but the '7/8 homing matches' headline must be read strictly as 'the per-tick ratio matches', with the wall-clock rate 1.46x too fast. Shared root cause with G-003 / T-007 (TICK_HZ=30 should be 20.508).

reasoning: Every number verified. FRAGUN loads the aimed gun-shell timer with LDB #40 / STB G$TMR(U) (WSGUNS.MAC:150-151); radix is 16 (WSCOMN.MAC:5 .RADIX 16, inherited by WSGUNS — no local .RADIX), so #40 = 0x40 = 64 decimal, no trailing period. FRAGUN also sets G$MOV = MOV$AM (:154-155), and MOVAM decrements G$TMR each game frame and kills the shot at <=0 (WSGUNS.MAC:476-480 DEC G$TMR(X) / IFLE / clear type+timer) => a 64-game-frame life. At the pinned 20.508 Hz game frame: 64/20.508 = 3.121 s. Ours ENEMY_SHOT_TTL = 64/TICK_HZ = 64/30 = 2.133 s (state.ts:230, verified). Divergence factor 30/20.508 = 1.463x too fast — exactly as claimed. The 64-frame COUNT is faithful; only the seconds conversion is wrong, because TICK_HZ=30 not 20.508. Shared root cause with T-007 (the state.ts:211-220 justification for TICK_HZ=30 — 'the cabinet's rate is self-timed by vector-list length and is NOT pinned by the disassembly' — is contradicted by the pinned 20.508 Hz game frame). class DIVERGENCE, recommendation fix, size s all stand. No numeric corrections.

The ROM player laser is genuinely hitscan and ours is genuinely a travelling projectile — verified end to end. VWLAZ (WSLAZR.MAC:149+) draws the beams as lines from the gun ports to the current site; collision resolves INSTANTLY the same frame: CLSLZ (space) picks min(CL.GDS gun-distance, CL.ADS alien-distance) recorded during object draw and jumps straight to HTSG/HTSA (WSLAZR.MAC:763-788); CL.ADS/CL.GDS are reset to 'none yet' every frame (:137-141). CLBLZ (trench) clips the beam to a fixed forward line whose endpoint is LDD #7000 (0x7000 = 28,672 units) 'FARTHEST FORWARD POINT' (WSLAZR.MAC:418, cited verbatim-correct) — a beam endpoint, not a projectile. There is no shot record, no per-frame position integration, no speed, no lifetime anywhere in the laser path. Ours gives every bolt a world velocity PROJECTILE_SPEED = 12000 u/s (state.ts:187) and PROJECTILE_TTL = 3 s (state.ts:163) and resolves by world-space sphere overlap while the bolt flies (TIE_HIT_RADIUS 250, ENEMY_SHOT_HIT_RADIUS 150 — both confirmed in state.ts). The finding's account of the tunnelling artefact and the 900->5000->12000 speed saga being consequences of a model the ROM lacks is accurate. Genuine, rewrite-scale structural divergence; recommendation wont_fix (deliberate playable modernization) is the right call. No corrections.
